revisionPolicy: Git commit history is canonical. This manifest records the last reconciled product revision; any documentation PR creates a newer document revision.

instructions.purpose: Machine-readable execution queue for AI CLI, AI providers, and VPS-assisted audit work.
safetyRules: Read the required documents before changing source or infrastructure.
Do not repeat a Closed or Resolved in Source scope without new regression evidence.
Do not mark an audit Closed based only on static analysis or CI unless its closure criteria explicitly allow it.
Do not claim staging or production validation without versioned evidence from a persistent environment.
For VPS_REQUIRED work, verify target host, environment variables, backup state, fixture data, and rollback path before running destructive commands.
Never record credentials, cookies, tokens, personally identifiable information, or credentialed URLs in repository evidence.
Update this manifest, the audit document, AUDIT_MASTER_REGISTER.md, ROADMAP.md, and AUDIT_CHANGELOG.md in the same PR when a material marker, status, or next action changes.

audits.nextAction: Keep audit evidence, roadmap status, handoff markers, change ledger, and provider handoff records reconciled in every material audit-related PR.

PR #93/#95/#96 are documented in the ledger. On persistent staging, validate web/API/worker topology, health/readiness, queue/storage contracts, and runtime configuration; version evidence before considering closure.

PR #97/#98/#100/#106/#116 are documented in the ledger; issue #107 is closed. Inspect the schema-v2 maintainability artifact and one further trend before proposing a package-specific ratchet; #111 is optional scoped refactor work.

PR #103 is documented in the ledger. On persistent staging, record authorization-negative, cross-tenant, retry/concurrency, public-route abuse, and reverse-proxy client-IP/rate-limit validation for the versioned AUDIT-3 source controls.

PR #104/#115 are documented in the ledger. Execute issue #112 on persistent staging: HTTPS cookie/session, origin/CORS/CSRF, ingress/TLS/headers, rate-limit identity, storage/log redaction, upload/storage, and rollback validation.

PR #71/#74/#75/#81/#82/#83/#85/#87/#92 are documented in the ledger. Use persistent staging to run historical-data preflight, real query-plan capture, cross-tenant negative tests, storage-cleanup recovery drill, and alert/log verification.
PR #118 made the staging probe evidence safe. Execute issue #108 on persistent staging, then implement issue #110 only for stable non-destructive browser journeys.
Document and exercise persistent-environment deployment, rollback, service supervision, observability, secrets, and release evidence.
Run and preserve a restore drill for PostgreSQL and object storage on a persistent non-production environment, including RPO/RTO observations.
Agree workload, latency, concurrency, and data-volume targets; then benchmark API, reports/exports, worker queues, and capacity on staging infrastructure.
Prepare UAT roles, acceptance scenarios, migration/cutover checklist, operator training evidence, and sign-off record in a staging-like environment.
